audit_items.proof_trace: All three allocation sites in qos_conf.c (nfp_flower_cmsg_alloc at L172, L241, L305) either check for NULL and return immediately, or pass the allocated skb unconditionally to nfp_ctrl_tx, which always absorbs ownership — freeing via dev_kfree_skb_any on DMA/WARN errors, queuing to r_vec->queue on ring-full, or handing to the TX completion ring. No other dynamic allocators (kmalloc/kzalloc/vmalloc) are used anywhere in the file.
